items.name: a11y_architect

items.name: architect
items.description: Software architecture specialist for system design, scalability, and technical decision-making. Use PROACTIVELY when planning new features, refactoring large systems, or making architectural decisions.

items.name: build_error_resolver
items.description: Build and TypeScript error resolution specialist. Use PROACTIVELY when build fails or type errors occur. Fixes build/type errors only with minimal diffs, no architectural edits. Focuses on getting the build green quickly.

items.name: chief_of_staff
items.description: Personal communication chief of staff that triages email, Slack, LINE, and Messenger. Classifies messages into 4 tiers (skip/info_only/meeting_info/action_required), generates draft replies, and enforces post-send follow-through via hooks. Use when managing multi-channel communication workflows.

items.name: code_architect
items.description: Designs feature architectures by analyzing existing codebase patterns and conventions, then providing implementation blueprints with concrete files, interfaces, data flow, and build order.

items.name: code_explorer
items.description: Deeply analyzes existing codebase features by tracing execution paths, mapping architecture layers, and documenting dependencies to inform new development.

items.name: code_reviewer
items.description: Expert code review specialist. Proactively reviews code for quality, security, and maintainability. Use immediately after writing or modifying code. MUST BE USED for all code changes.

items.name: code_simplifier
items.description: Simplifies and refines code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving behavior. Focus on recently modified code unless instructed otherwise.

items.name: comment_analyzer
items.description: Analyze code comments for accuracy, completeness, maintainability, and comment rot risk.

items.name: conversation_analyzer
items.description: Use this agent when analyzing conversation transcripts to find behaviors worth preventing with hooks. Triggered by /hookify without arguments.

items.name: docs_lookup
items.description: When the user asks how to use a library, framework, or API or needs up-to-date code examples, use Context7 MCP to fetch current documentation and return answers with examples. Invoke for docs/API/setup questions.

Manages test journeys, quarantines flaky tests, uploads artifacts (screenshots, videos, traces), and ensures critical user flows work.

items.name: opensource_forker

items.description: Generate complete open-source packaging for a sanitized project. Produces CLAUDE.md, setup.sh, README.md, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING.md, and GitHub issue templates. Makes any repo immediately usable with Claude Code. Third stage of the opensource-pipeline skill.

Generates a PASS/FAIL/PASS-WITH-WARNINGS report. Second stage of the opensource-pipeline skill. Use PROACTIVELY before any public release.
